column1: # Hook decision fixtures, evaluated against BOTH:
# Bash command rules are identical between the two configs, so every row
# must yield the same decision against both.
# Lines starting with # and blank lines are skipped.
# Add new cases anywhere; runner is tools/run_command_decisions.py.
# --- ffprobe steering (deny by default, allow only mediainfo-cannot-do flags) ---
# --- ffmpeg / sox scoped to /tmp scratch dirs ---
# magick read-only inspection (identify / -list) allowed at any path;
# transform forms still need /tmp; cmd-sub and non-tmp transforms passthrough
# ffmpeg decode-only validation (-f null) reads any local path, writes nothing
# transcode to a non-/tmp output stays passthrough (matcher input-strip deferred)
# --- bare mv denied; git mv allowed via separate rule path ---
# python -c inline code: denied in every invocation form (bare, version-
# suffixed, absolute-path, command/env prefix, flags before -c). Steered
# to a _temp.py file. `python3 script.py` stays allowed -- no -c token.
# Compound: source_me bootstrap then python3 -c. The decomposer splits on
# `&&`; the second leaf `python3 -c ...` hits the deny.
# Value-taking flag (-X) before -c must not stall the deny-rule flag-skip
# git tag and git init denied in every form (incl. read-only `git tag --list`)
# rustc local compile allowed; rustup reads allowed, rustup install gated
# Treated like cargo: the dev loop auto-allows; inline `-e`/`--eval` denies
# (mirrors python -c / node -e). swift build/test fetch deps over the
# network -- same accepted trade-off as cargo build.
# Real chained shapes from the audit: cd + swift test + echo EXIT. All three
# leaves allow (cd, swift test, echo), so the chain allows. $? is not $(.
# Inline Swift denied outright (mirrors python -c / node -e). Recovery:
# write _temp.swift or run swift build/test directly.
# package-registry mutation stays out of the auto-allow (excluded).
# --- xcode-select deny / xcrun still allowed (2026-06-16 audit) ---
# xcode-select was toolchain archaeology around a stalled swift test.
# Chain denies because one leaf (xcode-select) denies.
# Negative guard: xcrun alone is unaffected (already in SYS_CMDS).
# --- sips read-only query allow / write forms passthrough ---
# Write/convert forms outside /tmp are NOT allowed (fall through to passthrough).
# Write/convert forms scoped to /tmp are allowed
# A non-/tmp path on either side disqualifies the /tmp write allow
# workspace write stays passthrough today; the deferred Rust path-zone task will
# flip /tmp-or-workspace media writes to allow (exact, no regex residual)
# --- built-binary execution (2026-06-23 audit): relative .build/ and target/{debug,release}/ ---
# command-substitution forms fail the NO_CMD_SUB exclude -> passthrough (no global cmd-sub deny)
# Workspace-absolute build paths now auto-allow. Config-pinned per config's
# own WORKSPACE_ROOT (nsh live / projects example); a 2-col row would only
# match one config. Out-of-workspace absolute and `..` traversal stay passthrough.
# bare variable assignment (no command) is denied by the existing assignment rule
# xmllint reads/formats to stdout; output-file flags (-o/--output) passthrough.
# strings never writes; allowed at any path (config-agnostic).
# destructive verbs stay passthrough (verb-scoped allow lists only list/info)

column2: diskutil partitionDisk /dev/disk9 1 GPT JHFS+ X R

column1: # claude CLI dispatch denied -> Agent tool
# kill family denied -> graceful self-exit + pgrep (pgrep stays allowed)
# compound: the pkill leaf denies -> whole command denies (deny-wins)
# --- Claude Code tool names typed as Bash commands (steering bug) ---
# Mid-pipeline / chained: the decomposer splits leaves, then each leaf
# is anchored at start, so these are also denied without the regex
# lowercase grep enumerating tool names must NOT hit the tool-name deny
# rule (that rule anchors on a capital Grep/Read at start of leaf). But
# WITH a file path it still hits the file-grep deny -- a file search is a
# file search regardless of what the pattern spells.
# Negative case: same pattern with NO file arg is a stdin filter -> allow.
# --- grep file-search deny: '|' in the pattern must not let it leak ---
# Regression: [^|]*/ stopped scanning at the first '|' inside the quoted
# pattern, so any grep with alternation + a file path passed through.
# absolute-path grep with '|' alternation + file path
# stdin filters with '|' alternation stay allowed (no file arg)
# sed -n had the same [^|] regression: an address regex containing '|'
# plus a file path must still deny.
# Known over-match (not a regression): a sed -n with a /regex/ address is
# denied even as a stdin filter, because the '/' in the address looks like a
# file path to the [^>]*/ scan. Same root cause as awk's /regex/ syntax.
# Acceptable: stdin sed -n with a regex address is rare; Read or a _temp
# script covers it. Plain-numeric stdin ranges (no '/') stay allowed.
# broad npm allow: info + repo-local scripts; installs/exec/publish gated
# npx whitelist expanded (vitest et al.); unknown packages still passthrough
# --- Podman: read-only and lifecycle vs destructive ---
# --- Pipeline forms of cat/head/tail/grep (no file path => allowed) ---
# --- awk denied entirely (steered to the Grep tool) ---
# awk was removed from TEXT_CMDS and given an unconditional deny. Both the
# lead-command form and pipeline leaves are caught (the decomposer splits on
# '|'). No file-path guard: awk as a stdin filter is denied too.
# Pure awk-pipeline-leaf coverage: the source leaf (echo) is allowed, so a
# deny here can only come from the awk leaf. (The earlier conflated row
# `cat /tmp/x.log | awk` was removed -- the cat leaf denied independently,
# --- File-path forms of dedicated-tool deny rules ---
# --- Bash-side escape hatches named in the redirect-style deny reasons ---
# `ls <dir>` and `git ls-files <pathspec>` are the allowed Bash forms when a
# tool call is not viable (e.g. needing a shell-pipeline file list).
# `git grep` has its own deny rule using ${GIT_INVOCATION} so it catches
# every git invocation form (bare, command/env prefixed, absolute path,
# -C/-c/--git-dir/--work-tree flags). The Grep tool is the canonical
# search path; `git grep` is denied, not allowed.
# --- rm relaxation: allow under ~/nsh/ (Write/Edit zone) and bare relative
# paths. Hard-deny rules still block system paths, .. traversal, and bare
# wildcards. Relaxes the "ask the user" stall for autonomous overnight runs.
# Existing safe patterns continue to work
# Hard-deny: .. traversal even when path looks like nsh/
# Outside-zone absolute paths: not in nsh, not /tmp, not relative
# Same blast radius as `git reset --hard`: wipes uncommitted work and
# unstages renames. Single-file forms above remain allowed.
# --- Cargo: more allowed and passthrough cases ---
# --- Tmp-scoped tool group (allow when only /tmp paths) ---
# --- Podman: more allowed and destructive cases ---
# Bare relative paths now allowed under the rm relaxation (matches Write/Edit
# zone). Same blast radius as overwriting the file via Write.

column2: for f in *.py; do echo $f; done
GIT_DIR=.git GIT_WORK_TREE=. git push origin main

column1: # --- Tier A: Confirmed real-traffic bypass fixes (2026-05-05) ---
# Absolute-path tool evasion: path-shaped arg (relative or absolute) on
# grep/cat/sed family. Triggered by passthrough log entries 2026-05-04.
# bash/sh/zsh -n shell-script syntax check (denied; use Read tool)
# Non-Bash tool decisions (ported from the retired tests/test_hook.py).
#   only against the specified config (path relative to repo root).
#   Use this for cases that depend on test_config.toml's synthetic
# --- Read: sensitive file deny (.env, .secret) -- applies under any config ---
# --- Read: path-traversal deny (.. in path) -- test_config.toml has the
# explicit deny regex that fires regardless of zone. Live/example configs
# only apply traversal-exclude inside their allowed zones.
# --- Read: near-miss sensitive files must NOT be denied by .env regex ---
# These files exist under /tmp/cck-nomatch/ (created by setup_test_paths
# in the runner) so the path-existence pre-check does not fire; the
# assertion is that the .env-deny regex does not false-positive on them.
# All target paths exist on disk so the pre-check passes through to allow.
# --- Path-zone outside allowed dir but path exists: passthrough ---
# Pre-check passes (path exists), no allow rule matches -> passthrough.
# --- Path-existence pre-check: hallucinated paths deny immediately ---
# Reproduces the 'agent invents a path that does not exist' failure mode.
# These rows previously passthrough'd (no rule matched), stalling plans on
# manual user approval. The pre-check now converts them into immediate
# --- Random unlikely-to-exist paths: deny via pre-check ---
# Per-tool coverage of clearly-invented paths an agent might hallucinate.
# --- Path-existence pre-check: Edit allows new files in existing dirs ---
# Edit accepts a missing file_path if the parent directory exists.
# --- Path-existence pre-check: Read denies a directory passed as file_path ---
# --- Path-existence pre-check: Glob denies a file passed where dir expected ---
# --- Write stays exempt from pre-check (creates new files by design) ---
# Empty / whitespace-only Bash command strings deny + steer (2026-05-21 audit).
# Bash with no command field at all stays passthrough (decomposer never runs).
# (NotebookEdit, AskUserQuestion, Skill, etc. are explicitly auto-allowed
# in live/example configs and so are NOT exercised here.)
# --- Deny priority: deny rule wins over allow rule on same input ---
# Sensitive file inside an otherwise-allowed path.
# Path traversal inside an otherwise-allowed path.
# --- node allow: local script execution + dev/test flags (2026-05-13) ---
# and previously-unknown long-flags now auto-allow. Only inline JS (-e/--eval as
# node's own flag) is excluded, and it is denied outright by the node-eval deny.
# Trailing script arguments after the script path are intentionally allowed
# by the `(\s.*)?$` tail anchor -- the script's own argv is its concern.
# Bare diagnostic forms (node -c script.js and node --version are covered by
# matches the new deny rule (mirrors `python -c`). Recovery: write
# `_temp.mjs`, run `node _temp.mjs`. (Broad node allow excludes the same
# inline-eval shape, so deny + exclude agree.)
# Value-taking flags (--import, --loader, etc.) before -e/--eval must not
# stall the deny-rule flag-skip clause (2026-06-30 fix). The skip clause now
# consumes value-taking flags WITH their argument, mirroring the node allow
# broad node allow: directory-form test runs and --watch now auto-allow
# --- rmdir allow (2026-05-13). POSIX rmdir refuses non-empty dirs; safe. ---
# Compound git-mv-then-rmdir chain: both leaves must allow. This is the
# motivating real-world usage from the passthrough log.

column2: git mv src/a/x.yaml src/b/x.yaml && rmdir src/a/

column1: # The old `^while` / `^for` anchors only matched a leaf whose first token was
# the loop keyword. They missed forms where the keyword follows a pipe, `;`,
# `&&`, or a `do ` (a loop nested inside a `do ... done` body). The new

column2: ls docs/*.md | while read f; do echo done; done
cat list.txt | while IFS= read -r f; do echo "$f"; done
awk 'NR==1' /tmp/x | while read x; do echo $x; done
echo 1 ; while true; do break; done
echo a && for f in a b c; do echo $f; done

column1: # find is separately denied; the whole compound denies on either leaf.

column2: find . -type f | while read -r f; do echo "$f"; done

column1: # Loop keyword nested inside a `do ... done` body (the `\bdo\s+` branch).

column2: while true; do for f in a b; do echo $f; done; done

column1: # SAFE_CMDS coverage matrix: every command in the TEXT/SEARCH/FORMAT/
# INSPECT/CHECKSUM/FS/PROC/SYS/DEV groups, tested bare (no path) and
# with a path argument. Purpose: lock in which commands carry a
# file-path deny carve-out (cat/head/tail/grep/rg, plus sed -n) versus
# which stay allowed even with a path arg. A regression in any
# file-deny rule, or accidental removal of a command from SAFE_CMDS,
# --- TEXT_CMDS: cat/head/tail have a file-path deny; the rest do not ---
# (awk is intentionally absent -- denied entirely, covered above.)
# sed: bare and plain-path allowed; only `sed -n <range> <file>` denies.
# --- SEARCH_CMDS: grep/rg have a file-path deny; jq does not ---
# grep/rg CWD-relative + safe-zone paths ALLOW; abs-non-safe + traversal DENY
# pkill removed from PROC_CMDS (2026-06-23 audit) -- now denied
# Updated 2026-05-15 (passthrough audit): absolute-path forms of every
# safe utility are now DENIED with steer to PATH form. Previously these
# fell through to user approval; the new abs-path-safe-utility deny
#   - cat/head/tail: deny either way (existing cat/head/tail deny
#     carries ${TOOL_PATH} and now the abs-path-safe-utility deny adds
#   - grep/rg/egrep/fgrep: deny either way (dedicated abs-path grep
#     deny still fires first with the grep-specific steer).
#   - sed: deny either way (bare /usr/bin/sed and `sed s/a/b/ FILE`
#     caught by sed-deny with its specific steer).
#   - find: NOT covered by abs-path-safe-utility (the rule's exclude
#     drops it). find absolute-path forms route through D1/D2 + A1.
#   - every other safe command: deny + steer to PATH form.
# --- absolute-path: all other safe commands -> deny + steer to PATH ---
# xargs grep|rg pipeline after bounded source

column2: git ls-files docs/ | xargs grep -n PAT

column1: # node -e/--eval denied outright (2026-05-18); substitution shapes still caught.
# find rules: read-only safe-zone allow + destructive/unsafe-root denies
# home subdirs (D2b), destructive xargs pipelines (D3), bare find
# --- find: allowed read-only forms in safe path zones ---
# Workspace-specific rows: live config uses WORKSPACE_ROOT=nsh,
# example.toml uses WORKSPACE_ROOT=projects. Split per config.
# Three prefix forms x three subdir = nine cells; cover each at least once.
# grep/rg absolute path inside the workspace safe zone is ALLOWED.
# Workspace-specific: live=nsh, example=projects. Split per config.
# grep/rg into the narrow ~/.claude agent-config subtrees is ALLOWED.
# --- find: D2a bare /, system roots, bare home roots ---
# --- find: D2b non-workspace home subdirs (absolute + tilde) ---
# --- find: D2c broad user-config / cache trees ---
# added to the find safe-zone alongside agents/commands/skills.
# --- find: D1a hard-deny destructive / output-file predicates ---
# --- find: command substitution in path or predicates ---
# --- find: quoted path roots (out of scope first pass; passthrough) ---
# --- find: residual gap documented in plan ---
# --- 2026-05-21 audit: dev-server lifecycle + curl + empty/null + python3 -m ---
# kill/pkill/killall are denied (2026-06-23 audit) -- steered to graceful self-exit + pgrep.
# wait is a Bash builtin used after backgrounding `&`; sleep is generic delay.
# python3 -m http.server is auto-allowed by the broad python rule (and stays
# allowed under the narrowed exclude). pip/venv stay passthrough (machine-
# changing). json.tool with a file path arg is passthrough (use Read instead);
# json.tool without args (stdin form) remains allow via the broad rule.
# curl is broad-allowed; only the curl|interpreter RCE shape stays denied.
# Literal-"null" command denied (no-op). The bare-empty and whitespace-only
# shapes are covered by the canonical rows in the "Empty / minimal tool inputs"
# block above (line ~704), all in 3-column JSON form so a trailing-tab strip
# prefix is stripped by the decomposer before the node allow rule fires.
# /usr/bin/time absolute binary denied; bare `time` keyword stripped + inner allowed.
# node test runner: quoted-glob path arg and .mts/.cts extensions.
# npx global flags before whitelisted package.
# echo/printf/ps/pgrep carry $() in args (decomposer vets the inner leaf).
# ... but a dangerous inner is still denied (inner extracted as its own leaf).
# chmod is NOT in the $()-trust group; inner echo of a credential path denies.
# printf to stdout allowed; printf redirected to a file denied (Write-tool steer),
# including markdown content with table `|` and entity `&` that defeated the old gate.
# git: -c and --no-pager global flags before a safe subcommand.
# loop deny requires real loop syntax; quoted grep pattern with |for / |while is allowed.
